item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I started reading "Sleep Paralysis Demon Is Literal" I was sure I would hate this thing, but the fact that this demon actually married the dad and they seemed perfectly happy was a trip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you said stepdemon, I kinda expected something different. Either way, was very well-written, and I appreciate the feel-good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea what I just read.

But I enjoyed every second of it and I think it was oddly adorable.

I also think that headcanoning technology that allows sleep paralysis communication is hella clever.

+100000000000000000000000000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was cuter than I expected, it made me smile. One awkward-feeling part of this article was the thought-to-speech dialogue, it felt a bit clunky to read. But it doesn't take much away, especially assuming that in-universe, this tech is probably new and imperfect.

The idea of a sleep paralysis demon being a chill soon-to-be husband is both creative and adorable. Also, I have never seen a footnote that made me laugh until now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree I'd love to read more about this, but it's still nice to see an entry just get to the point and not overstay its welcome.

Also, it's totally possible to read this as a demon corrupting a soul via gay marriage since some might say God's not super into that. The fact that the demon gets to travel the world with his hubby is just icing.
Good article, heartwarming narrative, and delightfully creative. I do wish to ask though, why it is classified as "Keter?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so funny, and I can't tell if it's creepy, wholesome, or just surreal. Whatever it is, it's good - I lost it at "As of December 4th, 2015." Easily going in my top ten favorite articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The implication here is that because the researcher said yes, the demon retroactively married his father. This would be the only instance where this happened.

As for the 15 reported cases, that's because most people would just assume it's just a normal part of sleep paralysis and ignore it.

To reiterate, the demon changed history to marry Patricks' father.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like there's still too much missing here. If the Foundation found photos of SCP-4423 from the researcher's childhood, what about the other subjects? Is it just coincidence that the one person they could rig up with thought-to-speech just happened to have a history with him? If there are only fifteen recorded cases, why even bother with inducing sleep paralysis in a researcher—the odds of catching a manifestation are still phenomenal.

I'd like to know more about the other incidents, if the people involved had anything in common. Otherwise, I can't tell if this is "wholesome demon married this researcher's dad" or "wholesome demon married EVERYONE'S dad retroactively."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm tired out on demons actually being decent God-fearing folks as an attempt to subvert their mainstream cultural connotations. The site's already subverted them in this way several times over, and you don't properly explore Researcher Patricks' father willingly falling in love with a demon, which could make for some fantastic heart-warming humor that would overcome the cliche subversion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww, ain't that the kind of modern love story we need more of.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt additional logs or tests would detract from the story presented, which is unique amongst all other SCP-4423 events because the researcher was able to talk to the demon. You can't really test it and any other interview would be repetitive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, but I was hoping for a few more experiment logs/other things the demon has done. Still +1

item.title: From the foot of your bed
item.content:encoded:
Oh wow, this was a super surreal thing to write.

spoilers/meaning
shhh

Here's what happened (in my mind.)

When Patricks said yes, it enabled SCP-4423 to open a portal back in time to twenty-one years ago, where he went back and woo'ed William Patricks. No tricks, no deceit. Pure love. Due to paradoxes and whatnot (and the fact I hate time travel) this causality was limited to just William and SCP-4423, who would remain the only people aware that time progressed differently.

This is the only time this happened because nobody else was able to give him permission.

Where are they now?

Bill and 4423 roam the world on a lifetime of adventure.

Misty lives with Jack now.

Mrs. Arnsdale has taken to adopting street cats.

And you, the reader, upvoted this piece! Wow thanks!

Critter:
The Great Hippo
Simartar

Brainstorming thanks to RockTeethMothEyes and Cerastes
